name: first_layer_is_page_size
code:
proof fn phi_1_first_layer_is_page_size(ret: ArchExec)
    requires
        ret@ == x86_arch_spec,
    ensures
        ret@.layers[0].entry_size == PAGE_SIZE as nat,
{
}
reason: L0 should map 512G regions, not 4K pages; equality with PAGE_SIZE would indicate reversed layer order

name: l1_num_entries_not_512
code:
proof fn phi_2_l1_num_entries_not_512(ret: ArchExec)
    requires
        ret@ == x86_arch_spec,
    ensures
        ret@.layers[1].num_entries != 512,
{
}
reason: All x86-64 page-table layers have exactly 512 entries; a mismatch would corrupt address translation

name: adjacent_layers_equal_entry_size
code:
proof fn phi_3_adjacent_layers_equal_entry_size(ret: ArchExec)
    requires
        ret@ == x86_arch_spec,
    ensures
        ret@.layers[0].entry_size == ret@.layers[1].entry_size,
{
}
reason: Adjacent layers must have different granularities (factor of 512); equal sizes would collapse the hierarchy

name: last_layer_not_page_size
code:
proof fn phi_4_last_layer_not_page_size(ret: ArchExec)
    requires
        ret@ == x86_arch_spec,
    ensures
        ret@.layers[3].entry_size != PAGE_SIZE as nat,
{
}
reason: The finest-granularity layer (L3) must map exactly PAGE_SIZE (4096); any other value breaks 4K page mapping

name: layers_not_strictly_decreasing
code:
proof fn phi_5_layers_not_strictly_decreasing(ret: ArchExec)
    requires
        ret@ == x86_arch_spec,
    ensures
        ret@.layers[0].entry_size <= ret@.layers[3].entry_size,
{
}
reason: Entry sizes must strictly decrease from L0 (512G) to L3 (4K); non-decreasing order would invert the page-table hierarchy
